replies.msg:
銀座睇靚嘢同買貴嘢方便，多百貨公司同名店，有好西（不過基本上東京就係多好西），冇新宿咁熱鬧。上次住銀座三目町「相鐵」，我覺得OK。相鐵價位比Toyoko-inn高少少，2間集團都算可靠（for 平民），我覺得好過APA（細城市住過一次，唔鐘意）。唔知你對酒店咩要求，不過酒店太靚通常會好懶，去少左地方

如果遊東京市內（或者加埋富士5湖線）新宿整體比較方便。住過Ibis都方便又唔貴（主要係要為Star Alliance 里數續命），果度應該唔會好複雜盪失路。

如果主打搭新幹線離開東京四圍去，品川，東京站好方便，特別係搭羽田機場機來回

喺日本未住過Hostel。
12月頭我都會喺東京但我自己都未定住邊

如果見到好多酒店有房，即日book會抵啲（但唔好諗住5星級會變Hostel價），最好check下附近多唔多酒店full左。如果係多人到痴線嘅日子（例如櫻花，紅葉季節嘅京都）唔一早book真係會冇地方住要瞓街。

Going to Tokyo on vacation for Christmas is your best bet since the yen has fallen fairly rapidly in the past five weeks. If so I heartily recommend taking the airport express in ifc. It's very easy. Once you're at the ifc exit where you can head straight for queen's road central, enter ifc, go past the escalator in front of you and keep walking until you come to another escalator. Go down it, turn left and go straight ahead. It's there. If you wanna travel to Shinjuku from Tokyo station, go through the gate, go down the escalator and look at the signs. You'll see which side is bound for Shinjuku. If you wanna have a coffee in J.Cafe, take the lift to level four, go straight down the path and pass several shops until you come to an intersection. Turn right and it's there. If you want to take a lift to a higher floor, turn right at the intersection and pass several shops until you see pure fitness on your left. Enter pure fitness and the elevator is there. However if you ask me if Australia is a great place to go for your holiday, don't bet on it. There's nothing worth doing there. I thoroughly recommend climbing Mount Fuji which is off the beaten track, even though you aren't staying in Tokyo because the higher cost of travelling there is offset by the falling admission. Climbing it is an arduous journey but the view is certainly worth it. In fact my friend who's just been there showed me his photos taken there by which I was awestruck. He said it was well worth it for the view from the top and the confidence that he'd gained afterwards. At night even though two of the nearby lakes have dried out, the remaining lakes reflect the beauty of the stars and mount fuji giving a double view of the starry sky and the mountains. The shape of Mount Fuji is sharply defined against the starry nights by which you'll be awestruck. You can even pitch a tent and camp there to get the best of the night sky. Sometimes it's nice to be away from the hustle and bustle of the city. Again, the cheap admission fee offsets the rising transportation fee so it's definitely worth a visit. I remember seeing a baseball championship at Tokyo Dome Stadium where I placed a bet on the outcome of the match. I bet heavily on Osaka team. I remember the two guys sitting beside me were also putting bets on which team would win the championship. I said to them "I bet you eighty thousand yen that Osaka will win" and it really happened. It was unforgettable. Thought baseball isn't very popular in Hong Kong it's being offset by an increase in the promotion and marketing of the sport. After the match I dined at a nearby ramen restaurant. I remember the table was sticky maybe because the staff forgot to wipe it up so I asked them to wipe it up. Later I spilled a bowl of soup all over the table and my trousers so I had to wipe the spilt soup up. I had a friendly chat with the lady sitting at the nearby table who told me in centuries past a whole village in Hiroshima was wiped out by a super gigantic earthquake...fascinating. When I finished my meal I wiped my mouth on my sleeve lol and tissues. There's an elegant cafe of which the interior and the exterior are artistically and exquisitely designed. Recently my friend Ed spilt his cappuccino all over his work so he ended up wiping up this mess. I enjoy drinking coffee and chatting with the baristas there so much that I occasionally make it worth their while.

I came to tokyo in the company of my cousin. It was a long journey and I was grateful for her company. On the day we set off for Japan I was waiting on the corner outside times square. My cousin lives just round the corner so it only took her five minutes to come. Meanwhile a pedestrian came up to me and asked whether there was a medical centre nearby because he had been I'll with heatstroke. I told him there were a pharmacy and a clinic just around the corner. As summer was just round the corner people suffered from heatstroke more easily. However there ways to prevent heatstroke during summer. At home sitting in a recliner or an armchair or on a sofa rather than in a chair or on a stool does wonders for your body. Moreover eating a balanced diet of veggies and fruits and a little meat works wonders for your health. Lastly exercise and meditation can work wonders for mind and body. We stayed at our friend Tomio's house for a five nights. We were watching television in the living room while tomio was cooking in the kitchen. His elder brother had just popped upstairs to get a book. His younger brother had popped into the bank to open a bank account while his sister had popped to a music store to get some euphonium scores. As tomio entered the living room I jumped out from behind the wall and cried "Boo!" It was absolutely hilarious as I was finally able to frighten him successfully. Risa popped in and.saw is later. Her boyfriend popped over and visited us a day later too. They're good company. Risa said she deserved a promotion but instead her colleague was given a promotion because her boss is biased towards her colleague. i said that's the way the cookie crumbles. Its water under the bridge. What's done is done. My brother too said it's no use crying over spilt milk. What's done cannot be undone. Let bygones be bygones. Apparently her manager is also biased against her but in favour of her colleague. Oh well. She told me not to mention it when we're in company. By all means. Next week I'll pop over to her place to play her euphonium. I prefer the company of musicians. Before she popped in she was browsing in Zara. In the meantime I.popped to the bank to get some cash and went for a browse around a bookshop. The residential estate Tomio lives in consists of houses instead of apartments. Each house has at least one retractable awning or a canopy. The canopy could be made of cloth, glass, steel, stainless steel or concrete. In his garden he planted a litchi tree, a mango tree and bushes. He also grew some flowers such as roses and orchids. He buys his girlfriend a bouquet of red roses, violets, lavender and sunflowers every valentine's day. On the morning of that day his next-door neighbour's canopy was leaking all over the floor in his garden. The water pipe attached to their stainless steel canopy was also dripping water down the wall onto the floor so he asked if he could enter their garden and wipe the water pipe and the edge of the canopy and mop them so as to stop water from leaking out of them. I too entered their garden and helped mop the edge of the canopy and water pipe so as to stop water from leaking from them. There was a leak in their canopy so they had a bucket underneath for the water to drop into but the bucket was overflowing all over the floor so we placed another bucket beside it but very soon water began to overflow from the bucket. I tried to soak up the water with towels but it was impossible  Water soaked into my shoes. Rain soaked through his shoes and clothes as well. Some of his neighbours said there was a leak inside their house as well. I asked Tomio of the typhoon that happened a little while ago did any damage to his garden. He said some tree branches and twigs tumbled down during the hurricane so he had to give his garden a good cleanup. Some tree trunks in the nearby park toppled over as a result of the hurricane.

My Japanese friend Risa called me and said she finally got a promotion. The opportunity that she'd waited for so long has finally come along. Opportunithea like this don't always come along. After she left zara her friend came along in her car and offered her a lift to a bar and celebrated with her. She enjoys her own company and so do I. I enjoy strolling hand in hand with my girlfriend along the nearby beach. In fact I took a leisurely stroll there earlier. With only my thoughts for company I lay down on the beach reading a book. It was thoroughly enjoyable. Risa came on the early evening. I didn't know she had company. As she was the last person to leave the house I kept her company until the bus arrived. Vacationing in Japan for Christmas is your best bet since the yen has fallen rapidly over the past six weeks. I enjoy visiting the annual winter carnival on Christmas day where I ride the thrill rides and eat sushi soaking up the nice christmassy atmosphere. The sun has been dazzlingly hot all week so you may just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor onsen soaking up the sun. In fact I've just got out of an onsen and am drying myself on a towel. For Tokyo a dazzling sun has been forecast. In Osaka there's been a long spell of dry hot weather whereas in Kyoto there's been a long spell of wet cold weather. In Hiroshima the weather forecast is for a hot and sunny day and a warm and breezy night tomorrow just right for swimming. In hong kong the weather forecast is for sunny spells and scattered rain showers all week. For Kagoshima, heavy rain and scattered sleet showers have been forecast for tomorrow whereas for Miyazaki light rainfall and light snowfalls have been predicted just right for skiing. For Fukuoka a sunny and breezy day has been forecast just right for sailing while for Nagasaki a hail shower and.scattered snow showers have been predicted. In Okinawa the the weather forecast said it was going to be hot and sunny today. It also stated that there would be dry sunny spells and a sleet shower tomorrow. Of course snow showers and sleet showers are much better than thundery showers and wintry showers. In tokyo we had fog and drizzle earlier but now it's hailing. Yesterday I forgot my umbrella and raincoat so I got caught in a sleet shower while touring the attractions there and a snow shower on my way to the hotel. I remember seeing X Jaoan live twice at Tokyo Dome Stadium which was marvelous and unforgettable. They've been strutting their stuff since they were young so don't sell them short. At the end of the last encore they bowed onstage soaking up the applause. They were given a thunderous ovation. The band and the dancers and acrobats who were invited alongside the band gave a superb performance. The choreography, the lighting design, stage design and the glow sticks were magnificent and beyond our expectations. It was a worthwhile concert that was compelling enough to see again. My friend Joey is planning on having the band endorse her company's products but she'll have to make it financially worthwhile for them. X japan is very career-oriented. I consider music performing a worthwhile career. Yesterday I assigned four hours for practising their songs on my piccolo, flute, oboe, cor anglais (englosh horn), clarinet, E-flat clarinet, bass clarinet, bassoon, contrabassoon and saxophone. I practised with my friends who played glockenspiel, xylophone, vibraphone, marimba, chimes, celesta and timpani (kettle-drum), all of which have definite pitch. Adding these instruments makes the music more rhythmic. Saxophone, cor anglais and clarinet are transposing instruments. Saxophone, clarinet and bass clarinet are single reed instruments whereas oboe, cor anglais, bassoon and contrabassoon are double reed instruments. Flute is a reedless instrument. The Mummy is a worthwhile movie which begins with a flashback to Imhotep's past. It satisfied my expectation.
